varieties.name: Bevy 🌱 ❌
varieties.origin: University of Adelaide, Australia (composite of 9 semi-dwarf spring rye types)
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated spring rye; Semi-dwarf stature, increased seedling vigor, superior tillering ability, flowers 2 weeks later than SA Commercial
varieties.yield_notes: High yields in longer growing seasons, performs far better than SA Commercial in Mallee environments
varieties.availability: Southern Australian seed suppliers (Victoria, South Australia)

varieties.name: SA Commercial 🏆 ❌
varieties.traits: Heirloom open-pollinated winter rye; Traditional variety, early flowering, taller stature
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yields, prone to frost damage in early flowering

varieties.name: Wrens Abruzzi 🏆 ❌
varieties.origin: Georgia, USA, 1953 (selection from Italian Abruzzi rye brought to Carolinas in 1680s-1830s)
varieties.traits: Heirloom open-pollinated winter rye; Early maturing, upright growth habit, tolerates warm winters better than northern varieties, very hardy, adapted to southeastern US piedmont and coastal plains
varieties.yield_notes: High forage production in fall/winter, rapid establishment and growth

varieties.name: Danko (Dankowski) 🌱 ❌
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated winter rye; Excellent winter hardiness, fruit-forward flavor characteristic
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, top performer among open-pollinated varieties
varieties.availability: North American seed suppliers, specialty grain markets

varieties.name: AC Hazlet 🌱 ❌
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated winter rye; Medium-early maturity, excellent winter hardiness, resistance to lodging
varieties.yield_notes: High among open-pollinated varieties, top performer with Danko

varieties.name: Rosen Rye 🏆 ❌
varieties.traits: Heirloom open-pollinated winter rye; Historical Pennsylvania variety, candy-coated flavor profile, dynamic taste
varieties.yield_notes: Lower than modern varieties (33% of Danko yield)

varieties.name: KWS Brasetto 🧬 ❌
varieties.traits: Hybrid winter rye (F1); Short stature (4-10 inches shorter than open-pollinated), excellent standing ability, good Fusarium resistance, low ergot with PollenPlus technology
varieties.yield_notes: 42% higher than check varieties, 23% higher than Hazlet in Canadian trials
varieties.availability: FP Genetics (Canada), KWS distributors. Cannot save seed (PVP protected).

varieties.name: KWS Bono 🧬 ❌
varieties.traits: Hybrid winter rye (F1); Excellent drought tolerance, very large deep root system, shortest in stature among hybrids
varieties.yield_notes: Highest yields in University of Minnesota trials 2016-2018, best dryland variety
varieties.availability: KWS distributors, FP Genetics. PVP protected.
